sections.paragraph:
-

(1) to flow, to flow down, [“to melt,”] (see Pilel, Hiphil). To this answers the Arab. ماع Med. Ye. Figuratively to be dissolved with fear and alarm (compare מָסַס), Eze 21:20 Psa 46:7 Amo 9:5.

(2) transit. to dissolve any one, i.e. to cause to pine and perish, Isa 64:6.

Niphal, to melt away (used of a host of men), 1Sa 14:16. Figuratively to melt with fear and alarm, Exo 15:15 Jos 2:9, 24 Jos 2:24 Psa 75:4.

Pilel מֹוגֵג to cause to flow down, to soften, e.g. arid ground with showers, Psa 65:11, בִּרְבִיבִים תְּמֹגְגֶנָּה. Metaph. Job 30:22 קרי, תְּמֹגְגֵנִי תֻּשִׁיָּה “thou hast dissolved my welfare;” כתיב, תְּמֹגְגֵנִי תְּשַׁוֶּת “thou hast dissolved me (and) terrified (me).”

Hithpalpel, to flow down, to melt, Amo 9:13, hyperbolically, “all the hills shall flow down,” shall all, as it were, dissolve into wine and oil. Figuratively to melt with fear and alarm, Nah 1:5 Psa 107:26.
